evidence: Shane: 'Our original product was incredible for what it was, but it's kind of like a widget. It does its job really, really well, but it's not particularly innovative. And we sort of tapped out the amount of value that we can give our customers.'

V: 'Not all of us want to look this fucking free. Some of us want to look like we have more things to do than a fully empty calendar. So I actually have to like my VC calendar... I have to have all these workarounds to make it look more busy than it is.'
V: 'Over the years I've developed an intuition for how much I can help someone and more importantly how much help is even worth offering because sometimes it just ends up being more.'
Shane reveals Calendly's July launch: CRM + 'AI Executive System' for relationship management targeting 'people who sell their time' who 'don't have CRMs - using hacked Notion or free HubSpot'
Shane commits to introducing V to Tobe for potential senior role opportunity at Calendly
V building voice engine to beat Pangram AI detection: 'Even with voice analysis, content still triggers AI detection.' Creates recursive loop where improving generation requires improving detection.
V: 'possessed by the vibe coding equivalent of the holy spirit' - all-night session (7:58 PM to ~7:00 AM) building complete products including interview feedback tool 'in a single prompt'
Anthropic 'pulled the rug' on Claude Code access affecting Zo despite V paying $100/month for max tier. Every AI-native workflow built on platforms that can change terms overnight.

Built in single overnight session using Zo's new Stripe integration: accepts JD + interview transcript, generates structured feedback with grades, final assessment. Amanda tested: 'very, very decent, very constructive'
Decision: 'Release 3 podcast episodes as a batch rather than weekly drops. Weekly drops suggest ongoing series commitment. Batch release creates splash and more eyeballs.'

Ivor: 'The value they get in the first six months will be like... yeah. The value they'll have in six years time if they keep up with it will be wild. Those people won't just be users, they'll be like fans and advocates.'
V: 'Academies have their local towns, regions, catchment areas locked the fuck down. And that is why you can reliably generate talent. If you aren't able to do that, you don't survive as an academy.'
V: 'Companies will never invest in true AI for resume scanning. Because why would you process a resource where the ratio is like 99 crap for 1% signal. And even that could be totally fake now.'
Ivor validates V's thesis through context: in a world of AI-generated everything, the only reliable signal is direct interaction over time - Careerspan's conversational modality as the escape hatch
Ivor: new Head of Strategic Ops will reach out after joining (week after next) to facilitate Handshake introduction. V: 'Handshake is the actual perfect acquirer'
V: 'Original hypothesis VALIDATED: conversational modality is the correct way to capture human data' - strategic pivot explanation to Ivor about Careerspan evolution
V explains pivot: 'going B2C to job seekers under duress was problematic - crowded marketplace with dog shit auto-apply tools, hard to differentiate; loyalty but no revenue'
V: '~3-4K users, 1/3+ with multiple stories. Deep data unlike any other platform. All from curated communities (alumni associations) with zero marketing.'
